quiz.title: Unit 10 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Net Ionic Equations and Solution Classification
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Net Ionic Equations, Spectator Ions, Saturated/Unsaturated/Supersaturated Solutions, Solubility Rules, Conductivity, Intermolecular Forces

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: Na‚Āļ(aq) + Cl‚ĀĽ(aq) ‚Üí NaCl(aq) is not a net ionic equation because NaCl is soluble and remains as ions in solution. This equation doesn't represent a chemical reaction at all. The other options show actual precipitation or neutralization reactions with spectator ions removed.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: Zn(s) + CuCl‚āā(aq) ‚Üí ZnCl‚āā(aq) + Cu(s) is a full molecular equation, not a net ionic equation. It includes spectator ions (Cl‚ĀĽ) that should be canceled out in a net ionic equation.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: AgNO‚āÉ(aq) + NaCl(aq) ‚Üí AgCl(s) + NaNO‚āÉ(aq) is a full molecular equation. The net ionic equation would be Ag‚Āļ(aq) + Cl‚ĀĽ(aq) ‚Üí AgCl(s), with NO‚āÉ‚ĀĽ and Na‚Āļ as spectator ions.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: 2K‚Āļ(aq) + 2Cl‚ĀĽ(aq) + Pb¬≤‚Āļ(aq) + 2NO‚āÉ‚ĀĽ(aq) ‚Üí PbCl‚āā(s) + 2K‚Āļ(aq) + 2NO‚āÉ‚ĀĽ(aq) includes spectator ions (K‚Āļ and NO‚āÉ‚ĀĽ) on both sides. The net ionic equation should be Pb¬≤‚Āļ(aq) + 2Cl‚ĀĽ(aq) ‚Üí PbCl‚āā(s).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a net ionic equation?
questions.explanation: HCl(aq) + NaOH(aq) ‚Üí NaCl(aq) + H‚āāO(l) is a full molecular equation. The net ionic equation would be H‚Āļ(aq) + OH‚ĀĽ(aq) ‚Üí H‚āāO(l), with Na‚Āļ and Cl‚ĀĽ as spectator ions.

questions.question: A solution is saturated at room temperature, 25¬įC. Afterwards, it is cooled to 20¬įC with no change to the appearance of the liquid. How would we describe this solution?
questions.explanation: When a saturated solution is cooled and no precipitate forms, it contains more solute than normally possible at the lower temperature. This is called a supersaturated solution.

questions.question: A saturated solution of KNO‚āÉ at 20¬įC contains 32 g of solute in 100 g of water. If a student dissolves 15 g of KNO‚āÉ in 50 g of water at the same temperature, how is the solution classified?
questions.explanation: At saturation, 50 g of water can dissolve 16 g of KNO‚āÉ (half of 32 g). Since only 15 g is dissolved, the solution is unsaturated.

questions.question: The solubility of NaCl is 36.0 g per 100 mL of water. A solution is prepared by adding 9.0 g of NaCl to 25 mL of water. Classify the solution.
questions.explanation: For 25 mL of water, the maximum amount of NaCl that can dissolve is (36.0 g/100 mL) √ó 25 mL = 9.0 g. Exactly 9.0 g is added, so the solution is saturated.

questions.question: Which of the following tests can be used to distinguish between an ionic solution and most molecular solutions?
questions.explanation: Ionic solutions conduct electricity due to the presence of mobile ions, while most molecular solutions do not conduct electricity well. Conductivity tests can distinguish between them.

questions.question: Which type(s) of molecule(s) are polar solvents more likely to be able to dissolve?
questions.explanation: According to the principle 'like dissolves like', polar solvents are good at dissolving polar and ionic substances. Non-polar substances are not soluble in polar solvents.

questions.question: Which forces affect solubility?
questions.explanation: Solubility is primarily determined by the intermolecular forces between solute and solvent molecules. Intramolecular forces (bonds within molecules) are not broken during dissolution.

questions.question: Which of the following is the least soluble in water?
questions.explanation: Silver acetate is slightly soluble (moderately soluble) in water. Lead(II) nitrate and magnesium sulfide are soluble, and lithium phosphate is soluble because Li‚Āļ is a Group 1 ion. Silver acetate has the lowest solubility among these options.

scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing between saturated, unsaturated, and supersaturated solutions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review net ionic equations, solubility rules, and solution classification concepts.
